concern.description: What the contract asks for, what makes it impossible as specified, and what would need to change in the plan. Required (non-null, non-empty) when status is 'blocked' — enforced by this schema's if/then. Always null when status is 'implemented'.

blocked_on.description: Required when status is 'blocked' — there is no default; the role must set it explicitly (enforced by this schema's if/then). Omit it entirely when status is 'implemented'. 'contract': the contract itself is unsatisfiable as specified — settable by either role, and the value to use whenever the blocker can't be attributed specifically to the tests or the production code. 'tests' (implementor only, TDD): the implementor is confident the authored tests — not the contract — contradict the contract; the driver routes this back to the test author instead of retrying the implementor. 'implementation' (test_implementor only, TDD, and only on a repair pass): the test author verified the authored tests are correct and the production code — not the tests — is what must change; the driver routes this to the implementor instead of re-running the tester. It is inert during the RED phase (a 'blocked' first/red-not-confirmed authoring pass falls through to red_test regardless of blocked_on), so a red-phase block should use 'contract'. The per-role/per-phase restrictions above are prose-only — this shared schema has no role/phase context and enforces only that some enum value is present when blocked.
